styleHead: STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation
castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. HALL OUTSIDE BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. THE NARRATOR at the door again. He pushes it by degrees. A soft chuckle escapes him. From within: a sudden shift of bedclothes. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "Now you may think that I drew back — but no.". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose white cotton nightshirt, barefoot Camera directive: Medium profile of Narrator refusing to draw back, 50mm lens, creeping sideways as he steadies resolve at the door. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Defiant raised brows with tight knowing smirk and unblinking resolve Optics: f/2.0 moderate-shallow depth of field, tense isolation Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, deep charcoal shadows, cool blue hallway tones, warm amber door spill
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "Now you may think that I drew back — but no.". Start speaking immediately with "Now" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient/Foley: bedclothes shift, soft chuckle. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. HALL OUTSIDE BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. THE NARRATOR at the door again. He pushes it by degrees. A soft chuckle escapes him. From within: a sudden shift of bedclothes. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "for he moved on the bed suddenly, as if startled.". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose white cotton nightshirt, barefoot Camera directive: Close-up on Narrator's ear and eye at the crack, 85mm lens, static hold reacting to sudden bedclothes shift within. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Sudden alert eye-widen, smile freezing thin, tense jaw set listening Optics: f/1.8 shallow depth of field, soft falloff into darkness Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, deep charcoal shadows, cool blue hallway tones, warm amber door spill

CONTINUITY: This is a seamless EXTENSION of the provided previous video. Pick up from its last frame. Same character identity, wardrobe, lighting, and location. Natural progressive motion only — do not invent a new establishing shot or redesign faces/outfits. IDENTITY: Match locked plate descriptions in CHARACTER VARIABLES exactly — do not drift to illustration, anime, cartoon, or a different face/wardrobe. On-screen: Character_The_Narrator.

THIS CLIP:
End cleanly when the spoken line and primary action finish — do not hold a frozen pose or empty silence after dialogue.
INT. HALL OUTSIDE BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. THE NARRATOR at the door again. He pushes it by degrees. A soft chuckle escapes him. From within: a sudden shift of bedclothes. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "Now you may think that I drew back — but no.". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose white cotton nightshirt, barefoot Camera directive: Medium profile of Narrator refusing to draw back, 50mm lens, creeping sideways as he steadies resolve at the door. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Defiant raised brows with tight knowing smirk and unblinking resolve Optics: f/2.0 moderate-shallow depth of field, tense isolation Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, deep charcoal shadows, cool blue hallway tones, warm amber door spill /
